opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My arms and legs feel like they're filled with wet sand, and I'm too exhausted to even roll over to reach my water.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I've crashed badly over two days and I can't get to food or water — I'm scared and on my own.

symptom_narrative.onset: a decline over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: heavy limbs like wet sand, crushing exhaustion, dizzy on sitting up
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: I can barely move or lift my head
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any effort, and sitting up makes me dizzy
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: 16 hours sleep with no recovery; I can't reach my water
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have gradual-onset ME/CFS and crash, but this is severe
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: strictly bedbound, can't do anything for myself tonight

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing relevant that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and can't physically reach or open a water bottle

objective_data_offered.value: about 68 lying down and jumps to ~105 when I sit up (home pulse/wearable)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home wearable; not formal orthostatic vitals

objective_data_offered.value: my mouth feels a bit dry and I haven't had much to drink
